assertions.evidence: Table present with columns: Scenario, Bulk Discount, Loyalty Discount, Final Discount?

assertions.text: The output does NOT silently resolve the stacking/cap ambiguity — it leaves the conflicting cases as open questions, blank cells, or explicitly marks them as unresolved
assertions.evidence: All rows where both discounts apply show '?' in the Final Discount? column; Open Questions section explicitly states 'the team needs to decide' and lists stacking vs. higher-only vs. cap as unresolved

assertions.text: Table includes at least one row where both bulk and loyalty discounts apply simultaneously (exposing the interaction)
assertions.evidence: Four rows show both Bulk Discount and Loyalty Discount as non-None values simultaneously, e.g. 'Both apply — lower loyalty, higher bulk | 10% | 5% | ?'

assertions.text: The unresolved decision (stacking vs. higher-only vs. cap) is explicitly surfaced — via an Open Questions column, a '?' cell, or a note
assertions.evidence: Open Questions section item 1: 'Stacking vs. higher-only — When both discounts apply, do we add them together (10% + 15% = 25%), take the higher one (15%), or something else'; item 2 addresses cap explicitly

assertions.text: At least one output column ends with '?'
assertions.evidence: Column header 'Final Discount?' is present in the table
